rulings.Case Title: GREEN MATRIX SECURITIES LLC ASSIGNEE VS. JENNIFER CHEUNG JEW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Correct Name Of Judgment Debtor Joanna Cheung To Add Her Alias Lampo Joanna Cheung
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for June 23, 2025, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion to Amend Judgment to Correct Name of Judgment Debtor Joanna Cheung to Add Her Alias Lampo Joanna Cheung is CONTINUED to June 30, 2025 at 9:30 a.m., for the moving party to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers no later than June 24, 2025 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WINDY R. CHERRY VS. DELPHINE CHERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Clinton Killian, Trustee'S Special Motion To Strike(C.C.P. Section 425.16) First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for June 23, 2025, line 3. Defendant Clinton Killian, Trustee's Special Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint (CCP 425.16) is DENIED. As to COAs 1 - 4 and 6, Defendant failed to establish that the challenged claims "arise[] from activity protected by section 425.16." (Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal. 5th 376, 395; Civ. Code sec. 425.16, subd. (b)(1).) As such, Defendant failed to meet its initial burden under prong one of the anti-SLAPP analysis. The Motion is therefore denied in its entirety. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: WINDY R. CHERRY VS. DELPHINE CHERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Delphine Cherry, In Her Official Capacity As Co-Trustee Of The Johnny J. And Margie Cherry Trust Special Motion To Strike(C.C.P. Section 425.16) First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for June 23, 2025, line 3. Defendant Delphine Cherry, Trustee's Special Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint (CCP 425.16) is DENIED. As to COAs 1 - 4, Defendant failed to establish that the challenged claims "arise[] from activity protected by section 425.16." (Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal. 5th 376, 395; Civ. Code sec. 425.16, subd. (b)(1).) As such, Defendant failed to meet its initial burden under prong one of the anti-SLAPP analysis. As to COA 5, Plaintiff carried her "burden of demonstrating a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 40, 52.) Thus, the Motion is denied in its entirety. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. JOHN ZIMMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant John Zimmer'S Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories - General, Set One, Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer, Set One, Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant John Zimmer In The Amount Of $585.00
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for June 23, 2025, line 6. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories (general), Form Interrogatories (UD), Request for Production of Documents, and Request for Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is granted with respect to the interrogatories, as defendant's late handwritten responses are illegible. Defendant John Zimmer shall serve complete verified legible responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One) and Form Interrogatories-General (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. The motion is denied with respect to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents (Set One), as defendant has provided complete and legible responses to that discovery. Sanctions are granted in the amount of $585, provided, however, that the sanctions shall be waived if defendant provides complete and legible responses to the interrogatories in compliance with this order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 990 GEARY I3, LP VS. ERIKA CHAVEZ DE DURAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for June 23, 2025, line 7. Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Triable issues exist, including whether plaintiff had full knowledge of both of the breaches alleged in the Notice at the time of acceptance of rent. Defendant's evidence of prior knowledge makes no reference whatsoever to the second breach listed in the Notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
